title: Cozy Manchego Ramen
description: A comforting twist on traditional Japanese ramen, featuring rich manchego cheese and savory beef broth. This satisfying bowl is perfect for a chilly evening.
instructions: 2. Roast the pork bones in the oven for 30 minutes, or until fragrant.
3. In a large pot, combine the roasted pork bones, ground beef, shoyu soy sauce, and 4 cups of water.
4. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 1 hour.
5. While the broth is simmering, cook the ramen noodles according to the package instructions. Drain and set aside.
6. In a large skillet, heat the sesame oil over medium-high heat.
7. Add the sliced ginger and cook for 1-2 minutes, or until fragrant.
8. Add the green onions and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
9. To assemble the bowls, place a portion of noodles in the bottom of a bowl, then ladle the hot broth over the top.
10. Sprinkle the shredded manchego cheese over the broth, then garnish with sesame seeds and green onions.
